title: coprosmanthus pseudochinus var. daibuensis
intro: Coprosmanthus pseudochinus var. daibuensis is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the bitter orange or Daibu citrus. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In traditional herbal medicine, Coprosmanthus pseudochinus var. daibuensis is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
